Artificial Algae Algorithm

A bio-inspired, population-based metaheuristic optimization algorithm that models adaptive algal colonies to search complex solution spaces.

Overview

The Artificial Algae Algorithm (AAA) is a bio-inspired population-based metaheuristic for global optimization. Candidate solutions are represented as algal colonies whose adaptive behaviour guides the search across complex solution spaces.

Motivation

AAA provides a flexible search framework for problems where gradients are unavailable, objective landscapes are multimodal, or practical constraints make conventional optimization difficult.

Biological inspiration

The algorithm draws its conceptual inspiration from algae: their growth, adaptation to environmental conditions, and movement in response to resources. These behaviors are abstracted into computational search operators.

Algorithmic mechanism

Each candidate solution is represented as an algal colony. Fitness-based adaptation and colony movement produce a balance between broad exploration and focused exploitation of promising regions.

1. Initialize colonies

Create a diverse population of candidate algal colony representations.

2. Evaluate and adapt

Use fitness values to adapt colony properties and focus search effort.

3. Balance search

Generate new positions to balance exploration of new regions with exploitation of promising solutions.

Algorithm flow

  1. Define the objective function and constraints.
  2. Initialize colonies and calculate their fitness values.
  3. Update colony behavior using growth, adaptation, and movement rules.
  4. Evaluate candidates and preserve the best solution found.
  5. Repeat until the stopping criterion is satisfied.

Pseudocode

Initialize algae colony population
Evaluate fitness of each colony
while termination criterion is not met:
    Update colony size and adaptation values
    Generate new candidate positions
    Evaluate candidate fitness
    Select and retain improved colonies
    Record the best solution
return best solution

Applications

  • Numerical optimization
  • Engineering design optimization
  • Feature selection
  • Machine learning parameter optimization
  • Scheduling
  • Industrial decision support

Implementations

  • MATLAB reference implementation
  • Python reference implementation
  • Research prototypes and community adaptations

Citation & resources

Please cite the canonical AAA publication when using the algorithm in scholarly work. The citation material and implementation links below are maintained as dedicated resources.
